subject: Re: Gain reference for TIFF compressed EPU outputs
body:
Hi Shintaro,

This makes sense to me based on the way RELION docs
<https://relion.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Reference/MovieCompression.html#falcon4-eer>
say they handle .gain

The gain reference for EER has confusing convention due to historical
> reasons. When the movie is in the EER format and the gain reference is in
> the MRC format, RELION will divide raw pixel values with the provided gain.
> In contrast, if the gain reference is given in the TIFF format with the
> .gain extension (as written by newer versions of the Falcon software),
> RELION will multiply raw pixel values with the provided gain (as is done
> for K2/K3). In any case, when the gain is zero, the pixel is considered as
> defective. The gain reference can be 8K x 8K or 4K x 4K. If the size of the
> gain reference and the size requested by --eer_upsampling do not match, the
> gain reference is upsampled / downsampled.


Cheers,

Alister

On Mon, 3 Jun 2024 at 15:17, Shintaro Aibara <shintaro.aibara@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Dear Warp Community,
>
> I've wanted to check with you what to do with the gain references now with
> TIFFs outputted by EPU with the .gain format references.
>
> For EER, I have been taking the reciprocal of a MRC converted .gain file,
> and applying it with Flip Y for our Titan F4i. This worked for EER, but
> does not for TIFF.
>
> Would it be correct that for TIFF movie, the correct thing to do is to
> just do the MRC conversion and leave it as is (and apply with Flip Y).
> Empirically this seems to produce the correct output, but I wanted to put
> it by those who have more rational thought than myself.
>
> Thanks in advance,
> Shintaro
>
